title: Extraction of flagellum basal body complexes from Buchnera aphidicola
author: mschepers
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bs5wng7e

steps.title: Grow aphids from birth to fourth...
steps.instruction: Grow aphids from birth to fourth instar (10 days) and harvest from plants. For this protocol, we used 3-5g live aphids.

steps.title: Load aphids into sterile fine mesh...
steps.instruction: Load aphids into sterile fine mesh tea infuser or other sterile straining device.

steps.title: Submerge into 0
steps.instruction: Submerge into 0.5% NaClO solution for 1 min.

steps.title: Transfer and submerge into sterile MQ...
steps.instruction: Transfer and submerge into sterile MQ H 2 O at 4 o C for 1 minute.

steps.title: Repeat step 4 (Transfer and submerge...
steps.instruction: Repeat step 4 (Transfer and submerge into sterile MQ H 2 O for 1 minute).

steps.title: Remove aphids from strainer, placing in...
steps.instruction: Remove aphids from strainer, placing in a mortar kept at 4 o C. Add 20mL buffer A + EDTA (pH 7.5) and homogenize aphids w/ pestle.

steps.title: Pour homogenized aphids into sterile vacuum...
steps.instruction: Pour homogenized aphids into sterile vacuum filter equipped with a 100uM nylon filter. Collect homogenate on ice.

steps.title: Remove nylon filter and place aphid...
steps.instruction: Remove nylon filter and place aphid filter cake back into mortar. Repeat homogenization with an additional 20mL buffer A.

steps.title: Repeat filtration with new 100mM nylon filter, combining filtrate
steps.instruction: Repeat filtration with new 100mM nylon filter, combining filtrate.

steps.title: Centrifuge 100uM filtrate for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 100uM filtrate for 10 min at 1500g at 4 o C in a pre-cooled centrifuge.

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant. Gently resuspend pellet in 40mL cold buffer A with sterile spatula or wide-bore pipette tip. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Vacuum filter resuspension through a 20uM nylon filter
steps.instruction: Vacuum filter resuspension through a 20uM nylon filter. Collect filtrate on ice.

steps.title: Immediately continue filtration through a 11uM...
steps.instruction: Immediately continue filtration through a 11uM nylon filter, collecting filtrate on ice.

steps.title: Filter through a 5uM nylon filter, collecting filtrate on ice
steps.instruction: Filter through a 5uM nylon filter, collecting filtrate on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 uM filtrate for 30...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 uM filtrate for 30 minutes at 1500g at 4C in a pre-cooled centrifuge.

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant. Gently resuspend pellet in 100 mL sterile sucrose solution. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Use light microscope to check integrity of Buchnera cells. Cells are spherical, averaging 3uM in size.

steps.title: Begin to gently stir on ice
steps.instruction: Begin to gently stir on ice.

steps.title: Add 5mL lysozyme (2 mg/mL, freshly...
steps.instruction: Add 5mL lysozyme (2 mg/mL, freshly prepared in H 2 O at 4 o C).

steps.title: Add 10mL 100mM EDTA solution pH 7
steps.instruction: Add 10mL 100mM EDTA solution pH 7.5 and incubate on ice gently spinning for 40 min.

steps.title: Add 10mL 10% Triton X-100 (in...
steps.instruction: Add 10mL 10% Triton X-100 (in H 2 O) to lyse cells. Solution should turn from turbid to clear.

steps.title: Once solution is transparent, add 100mM...
steps.instruction: Once solution is transparent, add 100mM MgSO4 and 1 mg/ml RNase-free DNase. Incubate at room temperature, gently stirring, allowing DNases to degrade cellular DNA. Solution should become less viscous after 30 min, this step can be extended to an overnight incubation at 4 o C if DNases require more time.

steps.title: Raise pH of the solution to...
steps.instruction: Raise pH of the solution to pH 10 with 1N NaOH.

steps.title: Spin solution at 5000g in a...
steps.instruction: Spin solution at 5000g in a pre-cooled 4C centrifuge three times, saving the supernatant and decanting into a new tube each time.

steps.title: After three spins, transfer supernatant into...
steps.instruction: After three spins, transfer supernatant into a polyallomer centrifuge tube and spin at 30,000g for 1h at 4C in a pre-cooled centrifuge.

steps.title: Carefully decant or draw off supernatant and discarded
steps.instruction: Carefully decant or draw off supernatant and discarded. Cover pellet with TET (10mM Tris-HCl, 5mM EDTA, 0.1% Triton X-100, pH 8.0) and leave overnight at 4C to soften protein pellet. Buchnera protein is ready for downstream analyses.

steps.title: NaClO solution
steps.instruction: NaClO solution: 0.5%; 83.5 mL 6% NaCLO in 916.5 mL sterile H 2 O Buffer A : 25mM KCl, 35mM Tris base, 10mM MgCl2, 250mM anhydrous EDTA in 950 mL sterile H 2 O. Add pellets of KOH until anhydrous EDTA is dissolved/solution becomes clear. Adjust pH to 7.5. Autoclave, then add 50 mL filter-sterilized 2.5M sucrose. Sucrose solution: 0.5M sucrose, 100mM Tris-HCl. Raise pH to 8 and filter sterilize. Lysozyme solution: Prepare 2 mg/mL egg white lysozyme in sterile H 2 O. EDTA solution: 100mM anhydrous EDTA in sterile H 2 O. Add KOH until EDTA is dissolved/solution becomes clear. Adjust pH to 7.5. Triton X-100 solution: 10% w/v Triton X-100 detergent in sterile H 2 O. MgSO4 solution : 100mM MgSO4 in sterile H 2 O. RNase-free DNase: 1 mg/mL in sterile H 2 O. NaOH, 1N: Dissolve NaOH pellets in sterile H 2 O. TET Buffer: 10mM Tris-Hcl, 5mM EDTA, 0.1% w/v Triton X-100. Add KOH or NaOH to dissolve anhydrous EDTA, then adjust pH to 8.0.
